title: Calm & Stress Relief
subtitle: Release tension. Come back to yourself.

cues.text:
Find a comfortable position, sitting or lying down.
Let your hands rest gently wherever they fall.
Close your eyes... or soften your gaze downward.

Take a slow breath in through your nose...
and let it go, through your mouth.

One more time, in...
and out. Good.

Now let your breathing find its own natural rhythm.
You don't need to control it.
Just... let it happen.

Notice where you're holding tension right now.
Maybe it's in your shoulders. Your jaw. Your hands.
Wherever it is, just notice it.
You don't have to fix it.

With your next exhale, imagine that tension softening, just a little.
Not gone... just... lighter.

Let your shoulders drop.
Let your jaw unclench.
Let your hands open slightly.

You are safe right now.
In this moment, there is nothing you need to do, nothing to fix, nothing to figure out.
This moment asks nothing of you.

If your mind brings up a worry or a to-do, just notice it.
Gently say to yourself: not right now.
And return to your breath.

Breathe in calm... breathe out tension.
Breathe in ease... breathe out the weight of the day.

Stay here for a few more breaths.
Nowhere to be. Nothing to do. Just this.

When you're ready, take a slightly deeper breath.
Begin to bring gentle movement back, wiggle your fingers, roll your shoulders.
And when it feels right... slowly open your eyes.

Carry this softness with you.
You can return here, anytime.
